title: Sample Embedding with Epoxy Resin
author: Debora Ferreira Barreto-Vieira
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jqvcmw6

steps.title: Wash previously fixed cells in sodium...
steps.instruction: Wash previously fixed cells in sodium cacodylate buffer 0,2M in saccharose 7% for 5 minutes (3 times).

steps.title: Postfix cells in osmium tetroxide 1% for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Postfix cells in osmium tetroxide 1% for 30 minutes.

steps.title: Wash cells in sodium cacodylate buffer...
steps.instruction: Wash cells in sodium cacodylate buffer 0,2M in saccharose 7% for 5 minutes (1 time) and in distilled water for 10 minutes (2 times).

steps.title: Dehydrate cells by immersing the material...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate cells by immersing the material in graduated solutions of acetone as follows: acetone 15%, 30% and 50% (10 minutes each), acetone 70% in uranyl acetate 1% (30 minutes), acetone 90% (5 minutes) and acetone 100% in copper sulfate (2 times for 10 minutes).

steps.title: Infiltrate cells by immersing the material...
steps.instruction: Infiltrate cells by immersing the material in epoxi resin at a 1:3 ratio of acetone P.A. for 30 minutes and at 3:1 ratio of acetone P.A overnight.

steps.title: Embed cells by adding epoxi resin...
steps.instruction: Embed cells by adding epoxi resin to the eppendorfs containing the cells. Polymerization occurs at 60 ° C during three days.
